one_time_description: Adoption of PSAS 3450 Financial Instruments resulted in a one-time remeasurement gain of $2,291,105 on transition
economic_notes: Band owns timber licences and operates silviculture activities. Band owns 100% of Adams Lake Indian Band Development Corporation (GBE). Investments in All Nations Trust Company, ALDC, BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing, ALIB Construction Ltd., and Sexqeltkemc Enterprises Inc. Pierre's Point and Sandy Point operations noted.
notes: Audit is qualified for two reasons: (1) management had not completed assessment of PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations as of report date, and (2) GBE financial statements not prepared under IFRS as required by PSAS. Two consolidated subsidiaries are the Social Housing Fund Pre-1997 and Post-1996 programs. Adams Lake Indian Band Development Corporation is accounted for under modified equity method as a GBE. Silviculture liability of $2,246,433 is a significant non-standard liability. PSAS 3450 adoption created a $2,291,105 opening remeasurement gain. total_government_transfers estimated as ISC $8,773,373 + CMHC $1,286,613 + FNHA $2,841,442 + FNESC $693,795 + First Peoples Cultural Council $401,639 + Province of BC $4,298,588 + Secwepemc Child and Family Services $930,542 - note SCFS may not be government transfer = $19,226,012 less SCFS $930,542 if excluded; included here at $18,224,491 excluding FNSSA recovery and GBE income. OSR calculated as total revenue minus total government transfers.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
